title: Roasted Cauliflower Steak with Beet or Mint Yogurt Sauce
description: A roasted cauliflower steak topped with mozzarella and served over either a mildly sweet spicy beet yogurt sauce or a refreshing mint yogurt sauce.
steps: Slice the cauliflower into 1-inch thick steaks, keeping the stem intact.
Brush both sides with olive oil and season with paprika, garlic powder, black pepper, and salt.
Roast at 425°F (220°C) for 20 minutes. Flip and roast for another 8–10 minutes until tender.
Top with mozzarella and basil leaves, then broil for 2–3 minutes until the cheese melts.
• Beet Sauce: Blend the Greek yogurt, cooked beet, lemon juice, garlic, chili flakes, maple syrup (optional), and a pinch of salt until smooth.
• Mint Sauce: Blend the mint, cilantro, garlic, lemon juice, and salt into a paste, then mix with the Greek yogurt.
Spread the sauce onto a serving plate and place the roasted cauliflower steak on top. Finish with a light dusting of paprika.
notes: The beet yogurt sauce is mildly sweet with a gentle kick from the chili flakes.
The mint yogurt sauce is cool and refreshing and pairs especially well with a squeeze of lemon.
For extra protein, add grilled paneer before topping with mozzarella.
